title: Can no longer base an image on a local image
body:
### Issue Description

Until recently I was able to build an image that is based on another local image, that is itself based on a local image. Now I cannot due to:

```
STEP 1/17: FROM localhost/base-toolbox:latest
Trying to pull localhost/base-toolbox:latest...
WARN[0000] Failed, retrying in 2s ... (1/3). Error: initializing source docker://localhost/base-toolbox:latest: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
```

My `Containerfile` starts with:

```
FROM localhost/base-toolbox:latest
```

The `base-toolbox` `Containerfile` is:

```
FROM localhost/base:latest

LABEL \
    com.github.containers.toolbox="true" \
    name="base-toolbox" \
    version="1.0" \
    summary="Toolbox image that contains only the base image."
```

And the `base` `Containerfile` starts with:

```
FROM registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-toolbox:42
```

My `registries.conf` includes:

```
[[registry]]
location = "localhost:5000"
insecure = true
```

I can build the `base` toolbox fine, which is based on `localhost/base:latest`, so this suggests it might be due to chaining together multiple local images...?

From what I can tell, it's simply no longer recognizing and matching the `localhost` configuration in `registries.conf`. To be clear, this all worked perfectly up until around a month ago, so system updates must have broken things but it's hard to know when because I don't build these images often.

I have tried:
- `FROM localhost:5000/base-toolbox:latest` in the `Containerfile`
- `prefix = "localhost"` in the config
- checking `podman info --debug` to verify it "sees" the configured registry (it does)
- simplifying the build to not squash and not use the cache

Nothing has worked.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

I have described the setup above, but please let me know if more information is required.

### Describe the results you received

```
STEP 1/17: FROM localhost/base-toolbox:latest
Trying to pull localhost/base-toolbox:latest...
WARN[0000] Failed, retrying in 2s ... (1/3). Error: initializing source docker://localhost/base-toolbox:latest: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
WARN[0002] Failed, retrying in 2s ... (2/3). Error: initializing source docker://localhost/base-toolbox:latest: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
WARN[0004] Failed, retrying in 2s ... (3/3). Error: initializing source docker://localhost/base-toolbox:latest: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
Error: creating build container: unable to copy from source docker://localhost/base-toolbox:latest: initializing source docker://localhost/base-toolbox:latest: pinging container registry localhost: Get "https://localhost/v2/": dial tcp [::1]:443: connect: connection refused
```

### Describe the results you expected

Should be capable of building the image, should not be attempting to communicate with `https://localhost`

### podman info output


No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: Just confirming that if I base my image directly on `localhost/base:latest` rather than `localhost/base-toolbox:latest`, it builds and runs as expected. This is a viable workaround for me because `base-toolbox` is essentially an empty middle man.
Hi @mehrdadbn9, I'd like to work on this issue. Please assign it to me.

I'm having a similar issue.
I have a locally built image that's tagged as `www.personalrepo/ovs:3.4.0`

In another Dockerfile (ovn) I have
```Containerfile
FROM www.personalrepo/ovs:3.4.0 AS ovs
```

I can see the local ovs image before I try to build the ovn image:
```shell
$ podman images
REPOSITORY                                   TAG           IMAGE ID      CREATED            SIZE
.....
www.personalrepo/ovs                 3.4.0         b4b3cfd07a57  5 hours ago        276 MB
.....
```

But when I build the ovn image from the Dockerfile, it ignores the local image and downloads from the repo instead
```
[2/3] STEP 1/1: FROM www.personalrepo/ovs:3.4.0 AS ovs
Trying to pull www.personalrepo/ovs:3.4.0...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:3921a4ce5771384eb49524469c6e1fb5dabf74459925ca9f6eed82b03acab5fa
Copying blob sha256:eb498759dd0de4a2286e0418188ad005124154658439efcd24f2921e53efde19
Copying blob sha256:2f38a9d2193bb574cfb72cf9750ac3b3690091053bee7f9f3842b5710dd3961d
Copying blob sha256:955fd9d27731eff5bd0dac106855d66773bdb7679b570e7993b05d3748b814f5
Copying blob sha256:2fa6cd91d982f749c1b4fc35e073bb22cb386f815dd8949adbe5e479a1c94c76
Copying blob sha256:19824a44640bfabb4c4fb2abda441d6010beaec024dfc8375f6c4d88e9da3867
Copying config sha256:a16be1923620193ea8dacd14538cbd5b68594ce789f9ba4b95303844ea7cfcff
Writing manifest to image destination
WARNING: image platform (linux/amd64) does not match the expected platform (linux/arm64/v8)
--> a16be1923620
```

This doesn't work for me because the image on the repo is x86_64 and I need the locally built arm64 version of the image.

And after running the build, the local image has been untagged and replaced with the downloaded version from the repo.
```
$podman images
REPOSITORY                                   TAG           IMAGE ID      CREATED            SIZE
.....
www.personalrepo/ovs                 3.4.0         a16be1923620  4 months ago        267 MB
.....
```

P.S. www.personalrepo is not a real repo, I just would prefer not to post our domain here

@johnnyggalt, I attempted to create a reproduction script based on your description, but I wasn't able to replicate the issue. I might be missing some details. Could you please check if I missed anything? If not, can you verify that this script reproduces the issue on your end? I was unable to reproduce it on main with this script.

script:
```
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/28038


echo "Current registries.conf:"
if [ -f ~/.config/containers/registries.conf ]; then
    echo "--- ~/.config/containers/registries.conf ---"
    cat ~/.config/containers/registries.conf
elif [ -f /etc/containers/registries.conf ]; then
    echo "--- /etc/containers/registries.conf ---"
    cat /etc/containers/registries.conf
else
    echo "No registries.conf found"
fi
echo ""
echo ""

echo "0. Starting local registry on port 5000"
podman rm -f test-registry 2>/dev/null || true
podman run -d -p 5000:5000 --name test-registry docker.io/library/registry:2
sleep 3
echo "   Registry started at localhost:5000"

podman build -t localhost/base:latest -f- . <<'EOF'
FROM alpine:latest
RUN echo "This is the base image" > /base.txt
EOF

podman build -t localhost/base-toolbox:latest -f- . <<'EOF'
FROM localhost/base:latest
RUN echo "This is the toolbox image" > /toolbox.txt
EOF

echo "Build done"

podman push --tls-verify=false localhost/base-toolbox:latest docker://localhost:5000/base-toolbox:latest
podman push --tls-verify=false localhost/base:latest docker://localhost:5000/base:latest

echo "Push done"

podman images | grep -E "(localhost/)"

echo -e "\n   Checking registry contents:"
curl -s http://localhost:5000/v2/_catalog 2>/dev/null | grep -q repositories && \
  echo "   Registry catalog: $(curl -s http://localhost:5000/v2/_catalog)" || \
  echo "   Registry is empty or not accessible"

echo ""
echo ""

podman build -t localhost/final:latest -f- . <<'EOF'
FROM localhost/base-toolbox:latest
RUN cat /base.txt /toolbox.txt && echo "This is the final image" > /final.txt
EOF

echo "This should fail"

echo ""
echo ""

echo "Cleanup"
podman rm -f test-registry
podman rmi localhost/base-toolbox:latest localhost/base:latest localhost/final:latest
```  

Output:
```
Current registries.conf:
--- /etc/containers/registries.conf ---
# For more information on this configuration file, see containers-registries.conf(5).
#
# NOTE: RISK OF USING UNQUALIFIED IMAGE NAMES
# We recommend always using fully qualified image names including the registry
# server (full dns name), namespace, image name, and tag
# (e.g., registry.redhat.io/ubi8/ubi:latest). Pulling by digest (i.e.,
# quay.io/repository/name@digest) further eliminates the ambiguity of tags.
# When using short names, there is always an inherent risk that the image being
# pulled could be spoofed. For example, a user wants to pull an image named
# `foobar` from a registry and expects it to come from myregistry.com. If
# myregistry.com is not first in the search list, an attacker could place a
# different `foobar` image at a registry earlier in the search list. The user
# would accidentally pull and run the attacker's image and code rather than the
# intended content. We recommend only adding registries which are completely
# trusted (i.e., registries which don't allow unknown or anonymous users to
# create accounts with arbitrary names). This will prevent an image from being
# spoofed, squatted or otherwise made insecure.  If it is necessary to use one
# of these registries, it should be added at the end of the list.
#
# # An array of host[:port] registries to try when pulling an unqualified image, in order.
# unqualified-search-registries = ["example.com"]
unqualified-search-registries = ["registry.fedoraproject.org", "registry.access.redhat.com", "docker.io"]
[[registry]]
location = "localhost:5000"
insecure = true

# [[registry]]
# # The "prefix" field is used to choose the relevant [[registry]] TOML table;
# # (only) the TOML table with the longest match for the input image name
# # (taking into account namespace/repo/tag/digest separators) is used.
# #
# # The prefix can also be of the form: *.example.com for wildcard subdomain
# # matching.
# #
# # If the prefix field is missing, it defaults to be the same as the "location" field.
# prefix = "example.com/foo"
#
# # If true, unencrypted HTTP as well as TLS connections with untrusted
# # certificates are allowed.
# insecure = false
#
# # If true, pulling images with matching names is forbidden.
# blocked = false
#
# # The physical location of the "prefix"-rooted namespace.
# #
# # By default, this is equal to "prefix" (in which case "prefix" can be omitted
# # and the [[registry]] TOML table can only specify "location").
# #
# # Example: Given
# #   prefix = "example.com/foo"
# #   location = "internal-registry-for-example.com/bar"
# # requests for the image example.com/foo/myimage:latest will actually work with the
# # internal-registry-for-example.com/bar/myimage:latest image.
#
# # The location can be empty if prefix is in a
# # wildcarded format: "*.example.com". In this case, the input reference will
# # be used as-is without any rewrite.
# location = internal-registry-for-example.com/bar"
#
# # (Possibly-partial) mirrors for the "prefix"-rooted namespace.
# #
# # The mirrors are attempted in the specified order; the first one that can be
# # contacted and contains the image will be used (and if none of the mirrors contains the image,
# # the primary location specified by the "registry.location" field, or using the unmodified
# # user-specified reference, is tried last).
# #
# # Each TOML table in the "mirror" array can contain the following fields, with the same semantics
# # as if specified in the [[registry]] TOML table directly:
# # - location
# # - insecure
# [[registry.mirror]]
# location = "example-mirror-0.local/mirror-for-foo"
# [[registry.mirror]]
# location = "example-mirror-1.local/mirrors/foo"
# insecure = true
# # Given the above, a pull of example.com/foo/image:latest will try:
# # 1. example-mirror-0.local/mirror-for-foo/image:latest
# # 2. example-mirror-1.local/mirrors/foo/image:latest
# # 3. internal-registry-for-example.com/bar/image:latest
# # in order, and use the first one that exists.
short-name-mode = "enforcing"


0. Starting local registry on port 5000
af92bfe40553de0a56227af1b47205cd001bc074688fcc39a156efe0d6764769
```

@willgarrett610 I suspect your issue is related to a different one. I am currently investigating a similar problem. Could you please provide the `--log-level=debug` output of your build?

A reviewer has determined we need more information to understand the reported issue.  A comment on what is missing should be provided. Be certain you:

* provide an exact reproducer where possible
* verify you have provided all relevant information - minimum is `podman info`
* answer any follow up questions

If no response to the `needs-info` is provided in 30 days, this issue may be closed by our stale bot.

For more information on reporting issues on this repository, consult our [issue guide](ISSUE.md).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
